seoTitle: Mount Mulligan vs Mount Nathan — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Mulligan and Mount Nathan. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Mulligan vs Mount Nathan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Mulligan and Mount Nathan.
heading: Mount Mulligan vs Mount Nathan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Mulligan, Queensland and Mount Nathan, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Nathan has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Mount Mulligan (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Mulligan or Mount Nathan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Mulligan has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Mount Nathan scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Mulligan scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Nathan scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Mulligan or Mount Nathan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Nathan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
